## Decisions (ledger `queue8.decisions`, seq 0-27)

- **d-001** (predicate-surface, 2026-07-04): Option A: structured JSON expression tree is the only predicate surface, compiled to canonical CBOR; no SQL-string door; 0011a lowers to the same plan layer. Recorded in 0061 §14.
- **d-002** (profile-supersession-rewrites, 2026-07-04): Option (b): each profile's stale envelope/sequencer text and 'Recommended v1' section is rewritten during its own design session.
- **d-003** (spec-landing-order, 2026-07-04): Option (a): order confirmed; dogfood bootstrap (step 8 / prompt 4) may run in parallel at any time.
- **d-004** (naming, 2026-07-04): Tool namespace substrate_; crate loom-substrate; office profiles feature-gated; 'weave' rejected.
- **d-005** (substrate-extraction-timing, 2026-07-04): Substrate extracted now into specs/0061.md; profile-unique work pushed back into JIRAISH.md / PAGES.md.
- **d-006** (search-index-placement, 2026-07-04): Build on the 0033 search facade; scan-backed initial implementation behind the final tool contract.
- **d-007** (no-v1-contracts, 2026-07-04): All contracts are long-term decisions; staging is an implementation concern; 'Recommended v1' framing removed from 0061 and APPS.md.
- **d-008** (search-versioning-semantics, 2026-07-04): 0061 §10.1: scope latest | as_of | history; append-only content inherently historical; per-collection body-revision indexing policy.
- **d-009** (entity-versioning-hybrid, 2026-07-04): 0061 §9: CAS bodies, op-log identity, projected revision tables; VCS commits are storage/sync, not profile history.
- **d-010** (review-and-queue-scoping, 2026-07-04): Q1=B mechanical review pass, findings await owner; Q2=one queue only; G1→profiles (requirement), G2→JIRAISH+PAGES, G3→0061 open decision 7.
- **d-011** (shared-facilities-promotion, 2026-07-04): Attachments, mentions, watches, labels, saved queries promoted to 0061 §7.1 after auditing all four profiles.
- **d-012** (questions-on-ledger-not-queue, 2026-07-04): Bootstrap deviation: Planner host serves queue_append only (no reads); questions live on ledger collection queue8.questions until queue reads are projected.
- **d-013** (cross-profile-reference-grammar, 2026-07-04): 0061 §19 (four decision points): explicit kind:/@/#/bare-issue-key extraction only; late binding; verbatim text + references block; key-holder binds at sequence time; refers_to/mentions edges only. WEBISH audited same session; ADOPTION G4 resolved.
- **d-014** (rich-body-model, 2026-07-04): 0061 §9.1 + PAGES §5: block doc + sequenced deltas + publish snapshots; CRDT deferred; reject-stale-base + client rebase; epoch-key shred; UTF-8 byte offsets; prefer sequencer-allocated block ids; run-encoded marks; minimal primitives; range+quoted-text anchors; XHTML and ADF import; preserved macro-reference nodes.
- **d-015** (rich-body-model, 2026-07-04): Unicode policy = option (a) verbatim storage. No normalization on write; identity over code points as written; offsets over verbatim bytes; byte-exact import round-trip; clients must not normalize before submitting deltas; 0033/0061 §10 search projection folds NFC + case at index and query time. Resolves q-007. Recorded in 0061 §9.1 and §10.
- **d-016** (jiraish-session, 2026-07-04): JIRAISH design session batch (23 decisions, landing step 2; full list in _QUEUE8.md): §7.3 validation machine; §7.2 conflict matrix (lww/guarded/human_review); §20.1 custom fields; §4.1 issue keys (retired-alias redirects); §7.5/§22 sprint-release policy (single-active, atomic close, dual release sets); §25 Jira import (history synthesis, identity stubs, mirror+cutover, fidelity report). Substrate lifts: scope operating modes (0061 §7.1), content_type-tagged body revisions (0061 §9 / JIRAISH §20.2). Reconciliation stitched 0061 §9.1 as the canonical collaborative-editing content type.
- **d-017** (baseline-diversification, 2026-07-04): Rally items adopted (JIRAISH §24.1: configurable portfolio hierarchy, nested stories, `accepted` status category, Rally capacity model; test traceability committed future); Redmine import → requirement; PAGES = "Notion surface, Notion internals" (reframe in prompt 5); block_ref committed (0061 §16.8); build focus = Redmine + markdown tree + Notion + Asana + Jira + Confluence; Notion/Asana ahead of Rally; Asana analyzed (multi-homing ↔ §4.1 key tension); Obsidian import feasible; landing step 9 added.
- **d-018** (baseline-diversification, 2026-07-04): Obsidian/markdown-tree import promoted to build focus (prompt 5 step 8). Compression audit: Loom compresses at the storage frame below the digest boundary (0005: DEFLATE/LZ4, optional AEAD, ≥1KiB); no user-facing zip/gz/tgz tooling; the import framework owns archive-container ingestion (ADOPTION §1.3 note).
- **d-019** (lifecycle-and-roadmap, 2026-07-04): lifecycle standard library ships as forkable templates - `feature`, `bug`, `incident`, `design`; owner framing: a lifecycle definition is functionally a prompt for the AI assistant (LIFECYCLE §3). Roadmap health = computed default + assertion override, divergence displayed (JIRAISH §21). Scope membership = explicit `governed_by` edges only (LIFECYCLE §4). Prompts 6 (SLACKISH + G7 agent metering) and 7 (DRIVEISH + archive worker question) written; landing steps 10-11 added.
- **d-020** (driveish-design-session, 2026-07-04): DRIVEISH session batch (prompt 7, landing step 11; owner accepted all eight recommendations; full list in _QUEUE8.md): §4.1 folder index (verbatim + NFC/casefold collision key); §4.2 FastCDC chunking 512KiB/1MiB/4MiB; §7.5 deterministic conflict-copy naming; §7.4 metadata merge matrix (edit wins over delete; `path_cycle`); §7.6 advisory→mandatory editor leases (`lease_held`); §8.1 dehydrated stubs; §10.1 live-root pin set; §11.1 archive preview/extract in scope (keyed worker); §21 Drive/SharePoint import mapping; supersession rewrite done.
- **d-021** (slackish-design-session, 2026-07-05): SLACKISH session batch (prompt 6, landing step 10; full list in _QUEUE8.md): channel log = 0021a stream, indexes are projections (§4); one sequencer-owned ref per scope (§4.1); remote-tracking refs + 0035 outbox, no push (§4.2); retention = epoch keys + fact-preserving live set (§4.3); stable-id permanent URIs (§9.1); blind/keyed = envelope visibility rule, operation_kind is metadata, lifted to 0061 §3 (§2.1); presence/typing ephemeral, unread = head − cursor (§3.1); task claims = sequenced first-claim-wins + optional lease (§3.2). Owner reframing: AI-centric first; Slack import demoted requirement→candidate; chat bodies = 0061 §9.1 canonical blocks as cross-platform superset (§3.3); model fixes: pins + emoji registry (0061 §7, §3.4); import mapping pinned (§19). ADOPTION G7 resolved as 0061 §20 (budgets at sequencer+PEP+0015; anomaly pause+elicitation; kill+mute; agent_forensics traces). Supersession rewrite done; WEBISH §23 converted (resolves q-006).

- **d-022** (notion-asana-session, 2026-07-04): Notion/Asana session batch (prompt 5, landing step 9; full list in _QUEUE8.md): portfolio items are issues - taxonomy levels bind issue types, strict adjacency, `hierarchy_violation`, epic_ref→portfolio_ref (JIRAISH §21.1); `accepted` integration - complete=done∪accepted, guards compose, velocity_counts per-project default done (§8, §21.2); capacity per-(sprint,principal), points/hours split (§21.2); Asana import (§26) - membership edges + owner-chosen secondary keys (§4.1 amended), manual board mode (§7.4), `imported_computed` freeze (§20.1), rules/forms are API-less fidelity gaps, intake gap (§24); Notion import (PAGES §5.6) - API is source, databases → `database` structures (§21) on §20.1 fields promoted to 0061 §7.1, `date_range` primitive, status groups → categories, views → saved queries; block_ref pinned (0061 §9.1, resolves §16.8) - no caching (shred-safe), render-time cycles + depth 8, index-once-at-source, `transcludes` edges, section mode; reframe - callout/toggle canonical, surface vs import kinds (§5.1); Obsidian import (§5.7) - folders → container pages with folder-note promotion, wikilinks late-binding, .canvas → canvas structures.
- **d-023** (naming-and-grouping, 2026-07-05): suite named **Loom Studio** (`office.` retired; `work` rejected as suite name). Per-profile capabilities flat + grouped by metadata: `tickets` (was office.issues; `issues.*`→`tickets.*`), `pages`, `chat`, `drive` (office.drive/files mismatch resolved), `web`, `lifecycle`, `surfaces` (was apps/APPS.md→SURFACES.md); bundle capability `studio`. Grouping mirrors CLI COMMAND_SECTIONS (flat names + group tag; new Studio + Substrate groups); rooted prefixes rejected (0061 §15.1). Folder `specs/office/`→`specs/studio/`; 8 profiles + 0061 renamed, verified clean.
- **d-024** (search-engine-ladder, 2026-07-05): `substrate.search` is an interface, not an engine - Studio decoupled from how search happens. Ladder (0061 §10.2): scan baseline → Tantivy lexical (_QUEUE2 280b) → 0017 vector/semantic (embeddings via 0050; backpressure→flow control) → hybrid RRF → 0040 GraphRAG. Modes text|regex|semantic|hybrid|similar_to; `match_via` provenance; keyless falls to scan. `substrate.search` and cross-Loom `loom search` = same interface, two scopes, one ladder (§10.3). Tier-2 edge ports (OpenSearch/Tantivy, Qdrant/Pinecone) are egress skins over the same indexes. New spec 0062 (landing step 12, prompt 8); DP open: new spec vs extend 0033.
- **d-025** (unified-search-discovery, 2026-07-05): resolves the d-024 DP. New composition spec **0064** `unified-search` (NOT 0062 - that number was already Inference Model Downloads; 0064 is next free after 0063-ask-app); mirrors 0040 composition precedent; 0033 stays lexical facet; 0061 §10.3 + index point at 0064. Scope corrected to **whole-Loom** (not cross-Loom): one Loom; `substrate.search`/`loom search` same interface, `loom search` = whole-Loom with optional namespace/collection/facet narrowing (no multi-Loom federation); `loom fts` stays collection-scoped lexical. Fusion = **RRF-only rank-based** (k=60 default, per-query override), no cross-engine score normalization, raw scores display-only. Tie-breaks: `match_via` priority → `entity_id` → `as_of` root. Embedding: bodies+append-only default, per-collection opt-out; **async keyed index-worker enqueue-on-write** reusing 0033 N.6/N.8 + 0017 §5 + 0040 accelerator lifecycle (INDEX_NOT_READY, coalescing, incremental via 0003b/0040 diff); provider via 0050/0062 local-default; **keyed-only reconciled with 0061 §3 / SLACKISH §2.1** (blind stores opaque vectors + routes by envelope metadata, cannot embed/tokenize; keyless → scan degraded). Permission-scoped fusion = **pre-filter per rung then fuse** (ACL predicate pushdown; no existence leak; graph honors predicate). Decoupling: exact request/response signature, degraded always signalled, **signature-stability guarantee**. Edge ports: OpenSearch/Tantivy (_QUEUE2 280b) + Qdrant/Pinecone (0017) = egress skins over the **same** indexes the ladder uses (one index, two faces, one rebuild path); Tier-2 build must not fork a copy. DP1-DP8 recorded in 0064.
- **d-026** (meetings-design-session, 2026-07-07): MEETINGS schema and import batch: hybrid operation-specific payloads for identity, evidence, redaction, and import runs with generic normalized source sidecars; raw source spans use deterministic source-locator identity while derived chunks use allocated stable ids; first conformance set covers canonical schema plus minimum projection effects for redaction and import idempotency; official import command is `loom meetings import --profile <generic|granola-api|granola-cache|csv> --input <snapshot.json> [--dry-run] [--format text|json]`; normalized imports use a batch envelope with profile, source scope, coverage, cursor, folders, items, and sidecar digests; dry-run performs full validation and fidelity reporting without sequencing operations, writing projections, or changing aliases. Recorded in MEETINGS §8.1a, §8.1b, and §16.
- **d-027** (meetings-import-command-correction, 2026-07-07): Corrects and extends d-026: the stable command uses `--input-profile` for the normalized input shape and `--report-format` for report serialization: `loom meetings import --input-profile <generic|granola-api|granola-app|granola-mcp|csv> --input <snapshot.json> [--dry-run] [--report-format text|json]`. `--format` remains reserved for ordinary output serialization in other commands and is not used for the input adapter. Granola API omissions caused by API visibility limits, missing generated summaries, missing transcripts, processing state, access denial, or rate limits are coverage gaps, not deletions. Meetings discovered through Granola MCP, filesystem import, or local app/cache import are source observations from those input profiles. Raw Granola API, app, MCP, and filesystem sidecars are retained by digest without a source-imposed retention ceiling, subject only to Loom-side user/admin retention, legal hold, redaction, or shredding policy. Import checkpoint semantics remain an open MEETINGS decision point.
- **d-028** (meetings-import-checkpoints, 2026-07-07): Import checkpoints are full and input-profile-specific. Every checkpoint records common fields for source scope, observed ids, completed units, coverage gaps, retry windows, and resume state. Granola API checkpoints additionally record filters, cursor, page count, rate-limit observations, and inaccessible ids. Filesystem and local app/cache checkpoints record root digest, discovered file ids, and completed file count. Granola MCP checkpoints record connector session id, retrieved note ids, and known coverage limits. Checkpoints support duplicate-free retries, scheduled sync, bridge cutover, and Import Coverage explanations. This closes the MEETINGS import checkpoint decision point.

- **q-005** [open]: Schedule the JIRAISH design session (workflow machine, conflict matrix, custom fields, key semantics, Jira import, supersession rewrite); unblocks profile-level dogfood migration.

- **q-007** [resolved → d-015]: DP-1: body text Unicode normalization - resolved 2026-07-04: option (a) verbatim storage (identity over code points as written, NFC/case folding in the 0033 search projection, byte-exact import).
